findings.description: Clear pothole visible in the road surface where asphalt has broken away exposing aggregate/rubble base material. The hole is located at the edge of the roadway near the curb/sidewalk transition, with visible rocks and debris inside the depression. Jagged broken edges are clearly visible around the perimeter of the hole.

In the North-facing and Down-facing images, there is a visible pothole at the edge of the road near the sidewalk/concrete area. The hole shows exposed aggregate/rubble base material with jagged broken edges where asphalt has broken away. The hole appears to be filled with debris/rocks and shows clear depth depression into the road surface.

A pothole is visible in the road surface near the drainage/curb area. The hole shows exposed aggregate and base material with broken jagged edges where asphalt has been removed. The hole appears partially filled with debris/gravel and has a clear depression with visible depth, located at the edge of the roadway near the chain barrier.

debug_points.description: This is a well-maintained urban plaza area in front of Plaza Banco General in Panama City. The road surface is paved with square concrete tiles and appears to be in good condition with no visible potholes or significant damage. The gray/brown blur in the lower portions of several images is a Street View stitching artifact from the camera angle, not road damage.

This is an urban street in Panama City showing well-maintained asphalt with clear lane markings and directional arrows. The road surface shows normal wear patterns but no actual potholes with missing asphalt or visible depth. Some minor surface irregularities and patch repairs are visible but no holes.

The road surface appears to be in reasonable condition. There are some minor surface wear patterns and discoloration visible, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are present. The gray/beige areas visible in several images are either Street View stitching artifacts (the blurred gray patches in the lower portions of frames) or normal concrete/stone pavement on the sidewalks.

Urban street scene in Panama City showing paved road with standard asphalt surface. Road appears to be in reasonable condition with no visible potholes or holes with missing asphalt. Some minor surface wear and texture variations visible but no actual depressions or exposed base material.

This is a commercial urban street in Panama City with well-maintained asphalt paving. The road surface shows normal wear but no potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible. The street features modern paver sidewalks, concrete planters, and yellow bollards marking no-parking zones.

The road surface throughout all 9 images shows a mix of paved areas with brick/paver sidewalks and standard asphalt roadway. The asphalt appears worn with some surface irregularities and color variations, but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible. The gray/beige areas in the foreground of several images are Street View stitching artifacts, not road damage.

The road surface shows general wear and some minor surface irregularities but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The asphalt appears intact throughout all visible angles. There are some wet patches and normal road wear patterns but no holes with exposed base material or broken edges.

The road surface is well-maintained asphalt with visible lane markings. The brick paver sidewalk areas show normal wear but no potholes. The large gray/brown blur in the lower portions of multiple images is a Street View camera stitching artifact, not water or road damage.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface shows typical urban pavement with some wear patterns, patch repairs, and normal surface variations. The dark gray areas in several images are Street View camera stitching artifacts (featureless gray smears without discernible texture), not water pooling or road damage.

This is a well-maintained urban street in what appears to be a commercial district in Panama City. The road surface is dark asphalt in good condition with no visible potholes. There are some wet patches and minor surface wear but no holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material.

The road surface appears to be standard asphalt in fair condition. The dark areas visible in the south-facing image are shadows cast by delivery trucks and wet/damp pavement, not potholes. No visible holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are present in any of the 9 images.

This is an urban commercial street in Panama City with mixed road surface conditions. The pavement shows general wear, discoloration, and some rough patches but no actual holes with missing asphalt visible. The road has typical urban deterioration but maintains structural integrity.

The images show an urban street in Panama City with parking areas and commercial buildings. The large gray/brown areas visible in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts (blurred, textureless smears) from the camera's steep angle, not water pooling or road damage. The visible pavement shows general wear and patching but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges.

The road surface appears to be in generally acceptable condition. The asphalt shows normal wear patterns but no visible holes with missing material, exposed base layers, or broken edges characteristic of true potholes. The gray-brown blur in lower portions of most images is a Street View camera stitching artifact, not road damage.

This location shows a well-maintained brick paver sidewalk area in front of what appears to be the Bristol Hotel entrance. The road surface visible is asphalt in fair condition with some surface wear and minor discoloration but no actual potholes with missing material. The large gray/brown areas in the lower portions of several images are Street View stitching artifacts from the steep camera angle, not water pooling or road damage.

This is an urban street in Panama City near Calle 50 with commercial buildings and parked vehicles. The road surface shows normal wear with some minor surface irregularities but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth. The pavement appears intact throughout all viewing angles.

This is an urban commercial street in Panama City with paver stone sidewalks and asphalt roadway. The road surface shows normal wear but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. There is a drainage grate visible in image 2 which is intentional infrastructure, not damage.

This is a well-maintained commercial/urban street in Panama City. The road surface is smooth asphalt with no visible potholes or holes with missing material. The sidewalk features decorative brick pavers and modern planters. Some minor surface wear and staining visible but no structural damage.

The road surface appears to be in good condition. The dark areas visible in several images are shadows cast by vehicles (delivery trucks, cars) and the typical asphalt coloration with some oil staining, not actual potholes. The pavement shows normal wear but no missing asphalt or holes with exposed base material.

Urban commercial street in Panama City with modern paved surfaces. The road shows normal wear patterns and some surface discoloration but no actual potholes with missing asphalt. The pavement has typical urban wear including some rough patches and water staining but no holes with exposed base material or broken edges.

The road surface consists of hexagonal interlocking pavers (adoquines) along the edge/gutter area, transitioning to smooth asphalt in the main carriageway. The large dark area visible in the bottom half of multiple images is a Street View stitching artifact (blurry, textureless gray smear with no discernible pavement details), not standing water or damage. No actual potholes with missing material, exposed base, or broken edges are visible.

This location shows a well-maintained hotel entrance area (Bristol Hotel visible in Image 3) with red brick pavers, proper drainage channels with metal grates, and striped bollards. The road surface is clean asphalt with clear lane markings. No potholes or significant damage visible.

The road surface shows typical urban wear with some discoloration and minor surface irregularities, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The pavement appears intact throughout all viewing angles.

The road surface shows significant wear, deterioration, and patchy concrete/asphalt transitions, but no actual potholes with missing material and visible depth are present. The large gray areas in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts (featureless blurs) from steep camera angles, not water or holes. The concrete surface shows age-related degradation and uneven texture but maintains structural integrity.

Urban street in Panama City with parked vehicles on both sides. Road surface shows general wear and aging but no visible potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges. Some graffiti visible on walls but pavement is intact.

This is an urban commercial street in Panama City with paver/brick sidewalk areas and asphalt roadway. The road surface shows normal wear but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. There is a drainage grate visible in image 2 which is infrastructure, not damage.

Clear pothole visible in the road surface where asphalt has broken away exposing aggregate/rubble base material. The hole is located at the edge of the roadway near the curb/sidewalk transition, with visible rocks and debris inside the depression. Jagged broken edges are clearly visible around the perimeter of the hole.

The road surface shows significant wear, weathering, and surface deterioration with uneven texture and discoloration. However, no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The large gray/brown blurred areas in multiple images are Street View stitching artifacts from the close camera angle, not water pooling or road damage.

This is an urban street in Panama City with parked vehicles on both sides. The road surface is asphalt in generally good condition with some minor surface wear. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible in any of the 9 images.

The road surface shows typical urban wear with some minor surface irregularities but no actual potholes with missing asphalt. The pavement is intact throughout all visible angles. Some areas show normal aging and minor surface texture variations but no holes with exposed base material or broken edges.

Urban street scene in what appears to be Panama City (based on coordinates and '360Explora Panama' watermark). The road surface shows typical urban wear with some minor surface irregularities, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 images.

This is an urban street in Panama City with parked vehicles on both sides. The road surface shows general wear and some moisture/water pooling from recent rain, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The pavement appears intact throughout all viewing angles.

The road surface shows significant wear, deterioration, and uneven concrete/asphalt with surface discoloration and roughness. There is standing water pooling on the pavement in multiple images, but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The gray areas in the lower portions of images are Street View stitching artifacts (textureless blurs), not evidence of damage.

In the North-facing and Down-facing images, there is a visible pothole at the edge of the road near the sidewalk/concrete area. The hole shows exposed aggregate/rubble base material with jagged broken edges where asphalt has broken away. The hole appears to be filled with debris/rocks and shows clear depth depression into the road surface.

This appears to be a parking area or back alley with worn asphalt surface showing general deterioration, patching, and uneven surfaces. While the pavement shows wear and aging, there are no visible holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges that would constitute actual potholes.

The road surface shows significant wear, weathering, and discoloration but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The large gray/brown area visible in multiple images is a Street View stitching artifact (textureless blur at steep camera angle), not standing water or a hole. The concrete/asphalt surface is deteriorated but intact.

No potholes visible in any of the 9 images. The road surface shows general wear and some uneven pavement, particularly near the curb areas. Image 8 (Northwest) shows a deteriorated sidewalk/entrance area with cracked concrete near a gated parking lot, but this is sidewalk damage, not a road pothole.

The road surface appears to be dark asphalt in generally good condition. There are some wet patches and minor surface discoloration visible, but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are present. The dark areas visible are either shadows from nearby trees or wet pavement, not depressions.

The road surface appears to be in generally good condition with smooth asphalt. There are some dark wet patches visible in images 5 and 6, but these appear to be liquid stains or water on flat pavement rather than actual holes with missing asphalt. No exposed base material, broken edges, or actual depressions are visible.

The road surface shows general wear and some discoloration but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The pavement appears intact throughout all viewing angles, with typical urban road surface texture and some minor staining/weathering patterns.

A pothole is visible in the road surface near the drainage/curb area. The hole shows exposed aggregate and base material with broken jagged edges where asphalt has been removed. The hole appears partially filled with debris/gravel and has a clear depression with visible depth, located at the edge of the roadway near the chain barrier.

The road surface shows weathered concrete/asphalt with general wear and surface deterioration. There is a large gray blurred/smeared area visible in multiple images which is a Google Street View stitching artifact from the camera angle, not standing water or a pothole. The actual pavement visible shows cracks and rough texture but no holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material.

The road surface shows general wear and aging with rough texture and some surface deterioration, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The pavement has uneven patches and worn areas but maintains structural integrity without holes or exposed base material.

The road surface appears to be in good condition with smooth asphalt throughout all visible angles. There are painted road markings, designated parking areas including accessible parking (blue painted), and well-maintained curbs. No holes, missing asphalt, or exposed base material visible anywhere in the surveyed area.

The road surface shows a commercial/parking area with green painted concrete sidewalk area with yellow-bordered planters containing palm trees. The asphalt road portion visible is in fair condition with some surface wear but no actual potholes with missing asphalt visible. The green painted area shows weathering and paint peeling but no holes.

The road surface shows general wear and some minor surface deterioration but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. The green painted area on the left appears to be a pedestrian or parking zone with palm trees in yellow planters. The asphalt road shows typical wear patterns but no holes with exposed base material or broken edges.

Urban street scene in what appears to be Panama City. The road surface shows typical wear with some minor surface irregularities but no actual potholes with missing asphalt visible. The pavement is dark asphalt in fair condition with visible lane markings.

This is an urban parking area/side street in Panama City. The road surface shows general wear, minor surface deterioration, and some debris/dirt accumulation, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 viewing angles.

The road surface shows general wear and some uneven pavement texture, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The streets appear to be in fair condition with yellow-painted curbs and standard urban infrastructure.
